top_signals.title: US–Iran deal: Progress… but not quite lasting peace
top_signals.summary: After nearly 100 days of conflict, the US and Iran agreed on an interim deal. The framework includes removing the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz (by Iran and the US) within 30 days of agreement signing in Switzerland (19 June), and 60 days to pave the way for detailed negotiations toward the final peace ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: US Crude Oil Inventories Down Again, Losing 52 Million Barrels in 9 Weeks
top_signals.summary: The American Petroleum Institute (API) estimated that crude oil inventories in the United States fell by 8.33 million barrels in the week ending June 12. Analysts had expected a 4.5 million draw. In the week prior, US crude oil inventories fell by a rather large 9.119 million barrels. Although inventories have been falling rapidly for the last 2+ months, shedding 52 million barrels over the last nine weeks, US crude inventories are only down 1.4 million barrels so far this year, according to API data. Inventories in the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Final meeting of the Special Panel on child safety online as Eurobarometer confirms link between social media use and wellbeing
top_signals.summary: European Commission Press release Brussels, 16 Jun 2026 Today, the Special Panel on child safety online met for the third and final time. On 13 July, the Panel's Co-chairs will present recommendations to President Ursula von der Leyen on how to further strengthen the EU's trailblazing framework for the protection of minors online.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Samsung sees rising chip production requests from BYD, Google, AMD: sources
Grammys to add best Asian pop music performance category in 2027
top_signals.summary: The new Grammy category gives J-pop, K-pop and C-pop acts greater opportunities for international recognition.
